subject: Geology
title: Mining Methods
description: A micro-lesson that makes Mining Methods usable.

hook.text: Some mines plunge kilometers underground. Others strip entire mountaintops. The method depends on the deposit.

buildup.text: Surface mining removes overburden to reach shallow ore. Underground mining follows deep veins with tunnels and shafts.

discovery.text: Open-pit mines like Bingham Canyon in Utah are visible from space. It has produced more copper than any other mine.

twist.text: Mining disturbs land, water, and ecosystems. Acid mine drainage can pollute rivers for decades after a mine closes.

climax.text: Mining extracts essential materials but leaves lasting scars. Balancing need and damage is a geologic challenge.

punchline.text: Every metal has a landscape cost.

quiz.question: What is a major environmental risk of mining?
